name: Nortista/Amazônico Portuguese
notes: Nortista / Amazônico (Belém, Manaus). Northeastern-type open pretonic vowels /e/ -> [ɛ], /o/ -> [ɔ]. Coda /S/ palatalised to [ʃ,ʒ] (Belém chiado): Silva (2014:2, ALFAL XVII #4814) reports, citing Noll (2008), 'certo espraiamento dessas realizacoes para outras areas do Brasil, a exemplo de Belem e Macapa, no Norte, onde haveria um grau de palatalizacao similar ao do Rio de Janeiro'; the ALiB-based grouping in Mota, Jesus & Evangelista (2010), cited ibid., places Belem and Macapa (with Rio de Janeiro, Florianopolis, Recife, Manaus, Cuiaba, Salvador) among the capitals where the palatal variant predominates in BOTH medial and absolute-final coda position. Unlike the Northeast proper, the North has high /t,d/ palatalisation before /i/ (ALiB: the North is the region of most expressive palatalisation), so palatalisation is RETAINED here (base default). Nascentes (1953:25, O linguajar carioca) grounds the North/South macro-division of Brazilian Portuguese on pretonic-vowel openness, placing this amazonico area in the falares do Norte. Tupí/Nheengatu contact in prosody and lexicon.

sources.author: Callou, D. & Leite, Y.
sources.title: Iniciação à fonética e à fonologia (8th ed.)

sources.author: Cardoso, S.A.M. et al.
sources.title: Atlas Linguístico do Brasil (ALiB), vols. 1-2
sources.notes: Geolinguistic atlas; pretonic-vowel and palatalization isoglosses.

sources.author: Noll, V.
sources.title: O português brasileiro: formação e contrastes

sources.author: Silva, A. R.
sources.title: Variação fonética em capitais brasileiras: a ditongação diante de /S/ e as realizações fonéticas do /S/ em coda
sources.publisher: XVII Congreso Internacional ALFAL, João Pessoa (#4813-4818)
sources.notes: Downloaded and read directly (printed conference page headers #4813-#4818). Cites Noll (2008) and Mota, Jesus & Evangelista (2010, ALiB-based) placing Belém/Macapá alongside Rio de Janeiro among capitals where the palatal coda-/S/ variant predominates in medial AND absolute-final position -- the source for the Belém/Northern chiado modelled here.

sources.author: Nascentes, Antenor
sources.title: O linguajar carioca
sources.publisher: Organização Simões, Rio de Janeiro (2nd ed.)
sources.notes: Downloaded and read directly. Grounds the North/South macro-division of Brazilian Portuguese dialects on pretonic-vowel openness (p.25); places the Amazônico area in the falares do Norte.

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam).
